name: Sprint retrospective with root cause analysis
description: Evaluate the skill's ability to facilitate a structured retrospective, extract patterns from observations, and perform root cause analysis using the 5 Whys technique.
prompt: Run a retrospective for our sprint. Here are the observations from the team: (1) We missed the deadline for the search feature by 3 days. (2) Two critical bugs were found in production after deployment. (3) Code reviews took an average of 4 days to complete. (4) The design handoff was unclear, causing 2 days of rework. (5) Deployment pipeline failed 6 times during the sprint. Categorize these, perform root cause analysis on the top 2 issues, and propose improvement experiments.

assertions.description: Should perform root cause analysis using 5 Whys or equivalent technique to dig beneath symptoms
Should formulate improvements as testable hypotheses with measurable outcomes
Should identify specific metrics to track whether improvements are working
Should recommend assigning a single owner to each improvement experiment
Should categorize observations into types like process, tooling, communication, or technical debt

name: Metrics-driven improvement cycle evaluation
description: Evaluate the skill's ability to analyze team metrics, establish baselines, identify improvement opportunities, and design experiments with timeboxes.
prompt: Here are our team's DORA metrics for the last quarter: Deployment frequency: 2/week (industry median: 1/week). Lead time for changes: 8 days (industry median: 1 week). Change failure rate: 22% (industry median: 15%). Mean time to recovery: 6 hours (industry median: 1 hour). Analyze these metrics, identify the most impactful improvement opportunity, and design a kaizen experiment to address it. Include baseline, target, timebox, and success criteria.

assertions.description: Should establish a clear metrics baseline from the provided data before proposing changes
Should identify MTTR as a significant improvement opportunity since it is 6x the industry median
Should design a structured improvement experiment with clear parameters
Should set a fixed evaluation period for the improvement experiment
Should address the change failure rate as it is above industry median
